people_management.headline_findings: The list confuses three distinct phenomena: (a) the candidate IS a people manager, (b) the candidate reports to a manager, and (c) the candidate provides informal/technical mentorship — and the topic definition explicitly excludes (b) and (c). The largest-hit concept (Coaching/mentoring at 6.0%) is dominated by 'mentor junior' (n=551) and 'coaching' (n=333), which the topic definition itself flags as out-of-scope informal mentorship. Several high-hit role keywords ('Senior Manager' n=65, 'team leads' n=54, 'team leadership' n=123) fire predominantly when the term refers to the manager the candidate REPORTS to, to a peer the candidate COLLABORATES with, or to a generic soft skill. The 'Headcount and hiring authority' concept has many semantically vague phrases ('build a team', 'grow the team', 'expand the team') that fire on growth-narrative boilerplate rather than hiring authority. The strongest signal patterns observed in snippets — 'direct reports' as a noun phrase, 'line management', 'manage staff augmentation/onboarding/firing', 'compensation decisions/planning', 'people leader' style titles — are well-covered, but the list needs guards against possessive-flips ('reports to', 'reporting to a Senior Manager') and benefit-language ('your career growth', 'opportunities for career development').

drops.reason: Almost all 65 hits describe the manager the candidate REPORTS TO ('reports to Senior Manager', 'under the supervision of the Senior Manager'); does not signal the posting itself is a people-management role.
Plural form fires almost entirely on 'collaborate with team leads / partner with team leads' — the candidate is NOT the team lead in 4/5 inspected snippets.
Predominantly a soft-skill bullet ('Team leadership skills', 'cross functional team leadership') rather than an actual leadership responsibility; high false-positive rate at n=123.
By far the highest-hit keyword (n=551) but topic definition explicitly excludes 'informal mentorship'; 'mentor junior developers' is the canonical senior-IC bullet, not a people-mgmt signal.
Same scope concern as 'mentor junior' — IC mentorship is explicitly out-of-scope per topic definition; 4/5 snippets are senior-IC postings, not manager postings.
Same as above; 'building tooling, implementing best practices, and mentoring engineers' is the boilerplate Staff Engineer bullet, not people-management.
Bare 'coaching' (n=333) is far too broad: 'technical guidance and coaching to other team members' is peer-coaching by ICs; not specific to manager responsibility.
Often refers to the candidate's OWN career growth as a benefit ('excellent opportunities for career growth and mentorship'), not to developing reports.
Same possessive-flip problem: 'Career development -- internal mobility...' is a benefits bullet aimed at the candidate, not a manager responsibility.
Bare 'set goals' fires on strategic/program goal-setting ('set goals and metrics, track progress', 'set goals and closely collaborate with Data Analytics teams'), not staff performance goals.
Triggers on 'database performance reviews', 'high-scoring performance reviews' (the candidate's own), 'quarterly performance reviews for the customer' — only ~50% of n=17 hits are staff perf reviews.
Vague growth narrative ('architect... and build a team around them', 'help build a team focused on solving hard problems'); does not specifically denote hiring authority.
Same as 'build a team' — used as a startup-pitch generality ('You'll help build the team') rather than authority.
Both n=2 examples are about building team capabilities/practices, not hiring people.
False positives like 'Expand the team's knowledge', 'Expand the team's knowledge base on anything you may be SME' are about knowledge-sharing, not headcount.
Single hit is a job-search-coaching service, not a manager job; risks polluting with off-topic postings.
Boilerplate Amazon Leadership Principle that appears in IC postings ('Hire and Develop the Best' as a value, not authority); n=3 with low specificity.
Fires on descriptive uses ('the team size consists of four different teams', '5-20 team size' in candidate background); not specifically a management scope signal.
The single n=1 example explicitly states 'Span of Control: 0, Individual Contributor' — keyword fires on the negation of management.
2 of 3 hits are boilerplate compliance text ('failure to disclose may result in disciplinary action'); only the 'administering disciplinary action' phrasing is real.
Single hit refers to a software product that automates 'labor management and staffing decisions', not the candidate's authority.
Zero hits — the regex is anchored on a literal phrase 'team of <num>' that does not occur as written; coverage is already provided by 'lead a team of' / 'manage a team of' family.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Used as the role the candidate WORKS WITH or REPORTS TO ('Work with the Engineering Manager to establish...', 'reporting to the Engineering Manager'), or as a hybrid alternative ('whether as a Tech Lead, Architect, or hands-on Engineering Manager').
guards.suggested_guard: Require the term to appear in a posting where the title or role descriptor is itself 'Engineering Manager' (title match), or where the phrase is 'as an Engineering Manager' / 'Engineering Manager role' / 'we are seeking an Engineering Manager'. Exclude 'reports to <X> Engineering Manager' and 'work with the Engineering Manager'.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Often used in retrospective consultant CV descriptions ('experience leading a team') with no indication the posting itself involves people management; also appears in technical-lead-only contexts.
guards.suggested_guard: Pair with people-mgmt corroborator within +/- 100 chars (e.g., 'reports', 'manage', 'hire', 'performance review') OR restrict to forward-looking phrasing in responsibility lists.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Frequently denotes technical SME role ('SME and leading the team by example', 'leading the team strategy', 'leading the team through transitions') without people-mgmt responsibilities.
guards.suggested_guard: Require corroborating people-mgmt token (direct report, hire, performance, 1:1, headcount) in the same posting.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Often technical leadership without people-mgmt scope ('leads a team to write, deploy, and maintain software').
guards.suggested_guard: Require co-occurrence with at least one Performance management or Direct reports concept hit in the same posting.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Same technical-leadership pattern ('Leads the team to write, deploy, and maintain software', 'leads the team in code/design reviews').
guards.suggested_guard: Same corroborator requirement as 'leads a team'.

guards.false_positive_pattern: CV-flavored experience requirement that is satisfiable by an IC tech lead.
guards.suggested_guard: Pair with a quantified size ('led a team of <N>') OR an explicit management responsibility token.

guards.false_positive_pattern: 'Manage the team's workflow/backlog/server environments' — process or asset management, not people.
guards.suggested_guard: Exclude when followed by possessive ''s ' + non-person noun ('workflow', 'backlog', 'environment', 'project'); require following it by 'of <people>' or co-occurring people-mgmt terms.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Same possessive-flip ('managing the team's workflow', 'managing the team' as % time split with coding) — describes activity allocation rather than authority.
guards.suggested_guard: Same possessive-noun exclusion as 'manage the team'.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Used for technical objects ('supervises the design, development, and maintenance of BI data systems', 'oversees the architecture, coding...').
guards.suggested_guard: Require object to be a person/staff noun (employees, staff, engineers, developers, team members, direct reports) within 5 tokens after 'supervises'.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Used as a generic soft skill in skill bullets ('Team leadership skills', 'Team Leadership SME').
guards.suggested_guard: Drop (recommended) or restrict to phrases like 'Team leadership: <responsibility>' (heading form) and exclude bare 'team leadership skills'.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Object often a project/program ('lead and manage small projects', 'lead and manage project') not people.
guards.suggested_guard: Require object to be person/team noun ('a team', 'engineers', 'developers'); exclude 'project(s)', 'program(s)', 'pre-sales support', 'small projects'.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Often a peer-coaching skill bullet on Senior IC postings ('passion for mentoring and coaching engineers').
guards.suggested_guard: Require co-occurring people-management corroborator (direct reports, hire, performance review, 1:1) in same posting.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Strong signal overall, but appears in negations ('No people leadership on any roles', 'embodies servant leadership even in a non-people-management role').
guards.suggested_guard: Add negation-window check: drop hit if preceded within ~5 tokens by 'no', 'non-', 'without', 'not a'.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Same negation pattern observed: 'No people leadership on any roles – these are all hands-on keyboard engineers'.
guards.suggested_guard: Same negation-window check as 'people management'.

Closely related to 'staffing decisions' but with stronger specificity. Phrasing is canonical in the topic definition itself ('owning headcount, hiring decisions') and warrants direct keyword inclusion. (Conservative: confirm with future calibration.)

concept_redefines.issue: This concept is the topic's largest hitter (6.0% hit rate) but its top three keywords ('mentor junior', 'mentor engineers', 'mentoring engineers', 'coaching') overwhelmingly fire on senior-IC postings where mentorship is described as a peer/IC contribution. The TOPIC definition explicitly excludes 'informal mentorship' yet the CONCEPT's own definition undermines that exclusion by including 'mentoring' as a primary semantic anchor. This is the structural reason the topic's apparent prevalence is inflated.
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Either (a) split this concept into two — 'manager-led career development' (career conversations, performance feedback, individual development plans, talent development of subordinates) and 'IC mentorship' (which should then be excluded from the topic), or (b) tighten this concept to require an explicit subordinate-relationship token ('your team', 'direct reports', 'engineers reporting to you') within the same sentence as the mentor/coach verb. Drop the bare 'coaching' and 'mentor junior' family unless guarded.

concept_redefines.issue: Conflates (a) being a tech lead (technical leadership only), (b) being a people-managing team lead, and (c) collaborating with peer team leads. The verbs 'lead a team / leading a team' surface all three meanings indistinguishably.
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Require all 'lead/leading/leads + team' patterns to co-occur with at least one corroborator from {Direct reports, 1:1 meetings, Performance management, Headcount, Termination} concepts within the same posting. Alternatively, demote this concept to a 'weak signal' tier and only count when an additional people-mgmt concept is also present.

concept_redefines.issue: Mixes high-specificity hiring-authority phrases ('hire and onboard', 'interview loops', 'recruiting and interviewing', 'lead hiring') with low-specificity team-growth narratives ('build/grow/scale/expand the team') that fire on startup pitch boilerplate. The latter group inflates concept hit rate without indicating actual authority.
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Separate into two sub-concepts: 'Hiring authority (specific)' — recruiting, interview loops, hiring decisions, onboarding, hire & onboard, talent acquisition — and 'Team growth narrative (weak)' — build/grow/scale/expand the team. Only count the former for the people-management indicator; treat the latter as auxiliary.

concept_redefines.issue: All four current keywords are second-person verb phrases ('will report to you', 'reporting to you', 'report into you', 'managing direct reports'), missing the most common noun phrase 'direct reports' alone, which appears in many real management postings and is the strongest single-token signal.
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Add the bare noun phrase 'direct reports' (with optional possessive variants like 'your direct reports', 'their direct reports'). Also add 'N direct reports' / 'direct report' singular.

concept_redefines.issue: Concept has only 3 keywords with combined hit rate near zero; one ('staffing decisions') is a false positive and another ('disciplinary action') is dominated by compliance boilerplate. The strongest authentic phrasing observed is 'hire, retain... fire' embedded in a longer responsibility list.
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Drop 'staffing decisions' and guard 'disciplinary action' (require co-occurrence with people-mgmt terms in same sentence). Add 'hire, retain', 'administering disciplinary action', and 'firing decisions' as more specific replacements. Acknowledge that this concept may have inherently low recall and serve as a precision-only signal.

orchestration.headline_findings: The vocabulary's classical-orchestration concepts (Architecture, Task Decomposition, Workflow/Pipeline) are well-formed but overweighted by a few high-recall stems that double-count related concepts: 'architecture/architectural/architectures' alone produce >70% of the topic's volume, with 'architectures' frequently denoting neural-network/ML model architectures and 'orchestration' frequently denoting Docker/Kubernetes container orchestration. The Repository-Instructions concept is broken: 'skills' (12,294 hits, 63%) is essentially a pure false positive matching the universal 'skills' English word in 'communication skills', 'analytical skills', 'job skills' etc., and dominates the entire concept. Several agent/AI keywords collide with non-AI senses ('A2A' = application-to-application integration, 'function calling' = Python async function calling, 'red-teaming' = security red-teaming, 'multi-agent' / 'multi-agent coordination' = robotics/UAV multi-agent systems, 'checkpointing' = ML training checkpointing, 'session state' = generic web app state, 'RFCs' = ITIL Request for Change). The new agent-stack concepts (Context Engineering, Agent Harnesses, Multi-Agent, Memory, Eval/Steering) are otherwise on target with strong precision on AI-context snippets. Several genuinely-used tools and patterns are missing from the list: 'AGENTS.md', 'Aider', 'Cline', 'Roo Code', 'Foundry agents', 'Kiro', 'LangSmith', 'Promptfoo', 'tool-use', 'tool use', 'agent skills', 'task-state', 'agent communication', 'self-correction', 'Open Code', 'Agentforce', 'AIP'.

guards.concept: Repository Instructions and Agent Configuration Files
guards.false_positive_pattern: Plain English noun: 'communication skills', 'analytical skills', 'Job Skills', 'soft skills', 'problem-solving skills'.
guards.suggested_guard: If kept at all, require co-occurrence with 'agent', 'agentic', 'Claude', 'sub-agent', 'tool', or appearance inside a list of agent primitives (e.g., 'skills, prompts, and subagents') within a 60-char window. Otherwise DROP (preferred).

guards.false_positive_pattern: ML/neural-network sense: 'machine learning architectures', 'transformer architectures', 'RAG architectures', 'data architectures', 'LLM architectures'.
guards.suggested_guard: Exclude when preceded by 'machine learning', 'neural', 'model', 'transformer', 'LLM', 'RAG', 'data', 'reference' within a 4-token window; or require co-occurrence with classical software-architecture markers ('microservice', 'service', 'system', 'enterprise', 'distributed', 'event-driven', 'cloud').

guards.false_positive_pattern: Container orchestration: 'containerization and orchestration technologies (Docker, Kubernetes)', 'Kubernetes orchestration'. While arguably workflow-related, this is DevOps/infra, not workflow-design orchestration.
guards.suggested_guard: Exclude when within 30 chars of 'Docker', 'Kubernetes', 'container', 'k8s', 'EKS', 'AKS', 'GKE'. Keep when paired with 'workflow', 'pipeline', 'data', 'ML', 'agent', 'tool', 'process', 'job', 'task'.

guards.false_positive_pattern: ServiceNow / dev-tooling workflow: 'ServiceNow Workflow development', 'Workflow / AWE' (PeopleSoft), 'modern frontend build tools and workflows', generic 'development workflow'.
guards.suggested_guard: Exclude when preceded by 'frontend', 'build tools and', 'development', 'agile', or within 30 chars of 'ServiceNow', 'PeopleSoft', 'Jira', 'PUMs'. Keep with 'design', 'orchestrat', 'engine', 'automation', 'data', 'ML', 'agent'.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Same ServiceNow / generic-dev-workflow false positives as 'workflow'; also 'ServiceNow forms and workflows', 'modern dev workflows', 'configure workflows'.
guards.suggested_guard: Same guard as 'workflow' — exclude ServiceNow/PeopleSoft contexts and pure generic-dev-workflow uses; keep when paired with design/orchestration/automation/data/ML/agentic markers.

guards.false_positive_pattern: CI/CD/DevOps pipeline: 'Azure DevOps Pipeline and Release', 'CI/CD pipeline using Jenkins or GitLab', 'CI/CD Pipeline tools' — these are deploy pipelines, not workflow design.
guards.suggested_guard: Exclude when within 25 chars of 'CI/CD', 'CICD', 'Jenkins', 'GitLab CI', 'Azure DevOps', 'release', 'deployment'. Keep when paired with 'data', 'ML', 'ETL', 'ELT', 'orchestration', 'design', 'workflow', 'feature', 'inference', 'training'.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Causal-inference DAG: 'Directed Acyclic Graphs (DAGs) for causal modeling'; also 'DAG data model migrations'.
guards.suggested_guard: Exclude when within 40 chars of 'causal', 'Bayesian', 'directed acyclic graph' (when used as math definition), 'graph theory'. Keep when within 25 chars of 'Airflow', 'Dagster', 'Prefect', 'Argo', 'pipeline', 'workflow', 'ETL', 'task'.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Same causal-inference DAGs sense ('DAGs for causal modeling').
guards.suggested_guard: Same guard as 'DAG' — exclude causal/Bayesian/graph-theory context; keep when paired with Airflow/Dagster/Prefect/Argo/pipeline/workflow/ETL.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Application-to-Application integration: 'A2A/B2B Integration scenarios (Sync/Async)' — pre-LLM enterprise integration, not Agent-to-Agent.
guards.suggested_guard: Exclude when within 15 chars of 'B2B', 'B2C', 'integration scenarios', 'EAI', 'middleware'. Keep when within 30 chars of 'agent', 'MCP', 'LLM', 'LangChain', 'LangGraph', 'protocol', 'CrewAI', 'AutoGen', 'AgentCore', 'Bedrock', 'A2A protocol'.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Python async-programming sense: 'fluent with async function calling' — generic programming concept, not LLM tool/function calling.
guards.suggested_guard: Exclude when within 15 chars of 'async', 'asynchronous'. Keep when within 30 chars of 'LLM', 'GPT', 'OpenAI', 'agent', 'tool', 'RAG', 'structured output', 'JSON mode', 'OpenAI function calling'.

guards.false_positive_pattern: ITIL Request for Change: 'Testing, Creating RFCs and Migrating elements... ServiceNow', 'implementation of RFCs (application changes, infrastructure changes)' — change-management tickets, not engineering RFCs.
guards.suggested_guard: Exclude when within 30 chars of 'ServiceNow', 'ITIL', 'change management', 'production change', 'infrastructure change', 'CAB'. Keep with 'design', 'tech designs', 'author', 'write', 'draft', 'engineering', 'architecture', 'RFC process'.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Robotics/autonomous-systems: 'multi-agent autonomous systems' (UAV/Mission Software), 'single and/or multi-agent systems' (RL/decision making).
guards.suggested_guard: Exclude when within 30 chars of 'UAV', 'robotics', 'autonomous vehicle', 'reinforcement learning' (RL-only sense), 'mission planning', 'simulation', 'Fury', 'CCA'. Keep when within 30 chars of 'LLM', 'agent framework', 'LangChain', 'LangGraph', 'CrewAI', 'AutoGen', 'orchestration', 'workflow', 'role-based', 'planner/executor'.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Robotics RL sense: 'RL and DL for decision making and control of single and/or multi-agent systems'.
guards.suggested_guard: Same robotics-exclusion guard as 'multi-agent'.

guards.false_positive_pattern: UAV robotics: 'Multi-agent coordination of UAVs' appears verbatim across multiple Robotics SE postings — pre-LLM coordination problem.
guards.suggested_guard: Exclude when within 25 chars of 'UAV', 'drones', 'robotics', 'physical devices', 'CUDA kernels'. Keep when paired with 'LLM', 'context engineering', 'prompt design', 'agent handoffs'.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Security red-teaming: 'vulnerability management, red-teaming, and other security analysis functions' — classical infosec sense.
guards.suggested_guard: Exclude when within 30 chars of 'vulnerability management', 'pen test', 'penetration testing', 'security analysis', 'HPC', 'cluster admin'. Keep when within 30 chars of 'LLM', 'AI', 'model', 'evals', 'RLHF', 'agent', 'safety', 'jailbreak'.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Generic web-app session state: 'event-driven application development, session state management, web configuration' — pre-AI ASP.NET-era sense.
guards.suggested_guard: Exclude when within 30 chars of 'web application', 'ASP.NET', 'web configuration', 'cookies', 'HTTP session'. Keep when within 30 chars of 'agent', 'tool dispatch', 'sub-agent', 'long-running task', 'conversation', 'multi-turn'.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Potential collision with 'Android Development Kit' (older term); calibration snippets all show Google Agent Development Kit, but this should be guarded prospectively.
guards.suggested_guard: Exclude when within 15 chars of 'Android'. Keep when within 30 chars of 'Google', 'agent', 'agentic', 'Vertex', 'A2A', 'Bedrock', 'LangChain', 'CrewAI', or written as 'Agent Development Kit'.

concept_redefines.concept: Repository Instructions and Agent Configuration Files
concept_redefines.issue: Concept is dominated entirely by 'skills' (12,294/12,299 hits = 99.96%) which is a pure false positive. After dropping 'skills', the concept has only 5 keywords with a combined ~5 hits — operationally this concept will be near-zero coverage at the desired precision. The concept itself is well-defined and important (CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md/.cursorrules are real artifacts) but the named-file vocabulary is genuinely rare in JD copy at this date.
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Drop 'skills'; add 'AGENTS.md' and 'agent skills' (per ADD list) to broaden recall slightly. Accept that this concept will be a low-volume 'leading indicator' signal (<0.1% hit rate) rather than a base-rate concept; report it as such in the paper. Optionally widen by adding 'system prompts and CLAUDE.md context' regex-style multiword expressions.

concept_redefines.issue: Concept hit-rate is 52% — driven almost entirely by 'architecture' (35.4%), 'architect' (13.6%), 'architectures' (12.9%). This concept will essentially be 'is this a senior SWE posting?' rather than a discriminating orchestration signal. 'architectures' specifically pulls in ML-model-architecture noise (~2,500 hits with high FP rate).
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Either (a) move bare 'architecture'/'architect' to a broader 'senior-engineer base-rate' indicator and keep only the more specific keywords ('architectural decisions', 'high-level design', 'system design', 'ADRs', 'design tradeoffs', 'architectural patterns', 'distributed systems design') in this concept, or (b) keep them but explicitly normalize the concept hit-rate by reporting it separately from per-concept dominance metrics. Apply the 'architectures' guard regardless.

concept_redefines.issue: 'orchestration' alone (1,786 hits) is genuinely ambiguous between (a) container orchestration (Docker/K8s — adjacent to DevOps), (b) data/workflow orchestration, and (c) agent/tool orchestration. 'pipeline' alone is mostly CI/CD pipeline. Without guards, this concept conflates DevOps base-rate with the orchestration archetype the paper actually wants to measure.
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Apply the container-exclusion guard on 'orchestration' and CI/CD-exclusion guard on 'pipeline'. Consider promoting the more specific tool-named keywords (Airflow, Step Functions, Dagster, Prefect, Argo Workflows, Temporal-if-added) and de-emphasizing the bare stems when computing topic membership, so the concept becomes a 'workflow-engine fluency' signal rather than a generic-pipeline signal.

concept_redefines.issue: 'multi-agent', 'multi-agent systems', 'multi-agent coordination' all have meaningful collision with robotics/RL/UAV multi-agent literature, which is a legitimate but distinct technical domain represented in defense/aerospace SWE postings (Anduril Fury, Mission Software Engineer family). Without guards, this concept overcounts robotics-SWE postings as 'agent orchestration'.
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Apply robotics-exclusion guards (per GUARD list) on the three 'multi-agent*' keywords. Optionally split the concept into 'LLM Multi-Agent Coordination' vs 'Multi-Agent Systems (robotics)' if the paper cares about distinguishing them; otherwise just guard.

concept_redefines.issue: The concept's strongest-hit keyword 'checkpointing' is entirely ML-training checkpointing (not agent memory). verification.headline_findings: Vocabulary list is broadly well-targeted but contains several high-volume polysemous tokens that need guards: 'X-Ray' frequently fires on AWS X-Ray (correct, but also Jira X-ray test management, cross-listing concept), 'guardrail(s)' fires on AI safety AND on military weapons systems and unrelated business 'guardrails', 'monitors'/'alerts' fire on Workday-style notifications and product-monitoring devices unrelated to observability, 'observable' fires on the adjective ('scalable, observable systems') which is on-topic but 'on-call' fires heavily on customer support shifts and 'on call' has frequent COC-style false-positive title hits. The 'Reproducibility and artifact proof' concept conflates true reproducibility (formal verification, audit trails, reproducible builds) with generic 'logs/logging' and 'artifacts' which are observability and SDLC outputs. 'audit', 'auditing', 'auditor(s)' frequently fire on quality auditors (ISO 9001), data-usage audits, and engineering reviews unrelated to compliance verification. Coverage of modern AI evals is solid; missing items include 'eval suite', 'eval set', 'shift left' (already 'shift-left' present), 'pre-commit hooks', 'release notes', 'change failure rate', 'MTTR', 'MTBF', 'incident' (singular), and 'runbooks' which appear in observability snippets.

drops.reason: 3-letter token primarily fires correctly only when paired with UAT in IT shops; standalone 'sit' verb hits also appear ('we sit alongside engineering'). UAT already captures the same posting cohort; SIT adds noise relative to signal.

Singular 'log' hits 'log files', 'log analysis', 'Azure Log Analytics', 'log balancers' fragments — these are observability/operations, not reproducibility evidence. 'logs' and 'logging' already cover the legitimate observability hits and belong in observability concept anyway.

Adjective 'observable' fires on aspirational architecture phrases ('scalable, observable systems') which is signal for observability *as a design goal*, not actual post-deployment observability practice — and overlaps fully with stronger keywords ('observability', 'monitoring').

Verb 'monitors' fires on 'monitors and evaluates application performance' (verb usage) and noun 'Continuous Glucose Monitors', 'patient monitors' — high false-positive rate; 'monitoring' (not in list) or 'production monitoring' captures the signal more cleanly.
Fires on Workday 'Alerts and Notifications', business alert frameworks unrelated to observability. 'alerting' (already present) is the precise verb; 'alerts' the plural noun is too ambiguous.

Beyond AWS X-Ray, fires on Jira X-ray (test management, different concept), JFrog X-Ray (security scanning, different concept). Should be 'AWS X-Ray' to disambiguate; current bare form mixes three distinct topics.

concept_redefines.issue: Concept conflates three distinct things: (1) true reproducibility/formal-verification (formal verification, reproducible builds, reproducible research); (2) generic SDLC artifacts (test artifacts, code artifacts) which are deliverables not proof; (3) audit trails/logs which are compliance evidence. Generic 'log/logs/logging' and 'artifacts' dominate hit volume but are off-topic for the stated definition.
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Tighten to 'Reproducibility and formal proof' (keep: reproducibility, reproducible, reproducible build(s), reproduce, reproducing, reproducible research, formal verification, log evidence). Move 'audit trail(s)', 'audit log(s)' to Compliance and security verification. Move 'logging', 'logs', 'log', 'traces' to Post-deployment observability (where the snippets clearly point — observability pillars). Drop bare 'artifacts' as too generic; keep 'build artifact(s)' as the precise reproducibility variant.

concept_redefines.issue: Mixes AI-specific eval keywords (LangSmith, LangFuse, RAGAS, evals) with general-purpose terms (benchmarks, ground truth, red team, human-in-the-loop) that fire heavily in non-AI security and ML contexts.
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Either (a) split into two sub-concepts: 'AI/LLM evals (tool/method specific)' vs 'human-in-the-loop and ground-truth verification (general ML)', or (b) add AI-context guards on the polysemous terms (benchmarks, ground truth, red team, guardrails) so they only fire when in AI/LLM/model context. The current single concept blurs traditional pentesting red-teams into AI red-teaming, inflating the AI-eval count.

mentorship.headline_findings: The 'Direct mentorship language' and 'Pair / mob / ensemble programming' concepts are very clean and on-target; the rest of the topic suffers significant semantic drift. The biggest problem cluster is 'Code review as teaching' and 'Architecture and design guidance', where high-volume keywords like 'review code' (156), 'reviewing code' (37), 'constructive feedback' (155), 'design discussions' (209), 'peer review' (88), 'technical direction' (474), 'technical leadership' (1048), 'technical guidance' (518), and 'raise the bar' (70) overwhelmingly fire on generic seniority/QA-gate language with no teaching framing. 'Onboarding and ramp-up' is polluted by data/tool/equipment onboarding ('onboard datasets', 'on-board sensors', 'integrations onboarded'). 'Explicit learning culture' is dominated by candidate-trait phrases ('growth mindset', 'intellectual curiosity', 'continuous learning') that describe the applicant rather than the team's mentorship environment. 'Teaching and knowledge transfer' has a small set of high-FP terms ('teach', 'teaching') that match TA experience and presentation skills. Recommend strict drops for ambiguous singletons and concept-level redefinitions for code-review/architecture/learning-culture.

concept_redefines.issue: The concept is bleeding heavily into Verification semantics. The high-volume keywords ('review code', 'reviewing code', 'peer review', 'constructive feedback', 'design discussions', 'pr reviews', 'code-reviews') fire overwhelmingly on neutral correctness-gate language with no teaching/feedback/growth framing. Concept hit rate (3.68%) is dominated by these false-positive-prone signals; the genuinely teaching-framed phrases ('thoughtful code review', 'review that teaches', 'code review that teaches', 'deep-dive code reviews') hit only ~5 postings combined.
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Restrict the concept to phrases that explicitly couple review with teaching/feedback/growth language: keep 'thoughtful code review', 'review that teaches', 'code review that teaches', 'deep-dive code reviews', 'thoughtful feedback'. For the broad terms ('review code', 'peer review', 'code reviews'), require co-occurrence within the same sentence with mentor|teach|grow|junior|feedback|learn — implement as compound patterns rather than standalone keywords. Move the bare review terms to Verification.

concept_redefines.issue: Mixes two distinct senior-IC behaviors: (a) shaping technical decisions for/with other engineers (mentorship-adjacent) and (b) plain architecture/strategy work (not mentorship). Keywords like 'technical direction' (474), 'establish best practices' (55), 'define best practices' (27), 'promote best practices' (47), 'drive architecture' (27), 'design docs' (34), 'shape the architecture' (8), 'define standards' (21) all describe what a senior IC does, not necessarily teaching/growing others.
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Split into two narrower clusters: (1) 'Architecture review/critique as guidance' — keep only phrases that imply review-of-others' work or co-design with others ('architectural guidance', 'architecture guidance', 'design guidance', 'architectural review(s)' when paired with mentoring); (2) drop the pure-strategy keywords ('technical direction', 'set technical direction', 'shape the architecture', 'drive design decisions', 'drive architecture', 'design docs', 'establish patterns') from mentorship — these belong in a separate Senior-IC-scope topic. Keep 'raise the technical bar', 'raise the engineering bar', 'raise engineering standards' only with engineer-target proximity guard.

concept_redefines.issue: Concept conflates team-culture statements ('we have a learning culture') with candidate-trait requirements ('you have a growth mindset'). Most high-volume hits ('continuous learning', 'growth mindset', 'intellectual curiosity', 'intellectually curious', 'lifelong learner') are candidate traits — they describe who the company wants to hire, not whether the team mentors.
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Restrict concept to keywords that are unambiguously team/company-as-subject: 'culture of learning', 'learning culture', 'high-trust', 'psychological safety', 'blameless postmortem', 'blameless post-mortem', 'learn from each other', 'foster growth' (already in Coaching), 'learn from mistakes'. Drop or move all candidate-trait keywords listed in DROPs. Optionally rename to 'Team learning culture statements' to enforce the framing.

performance.headline_findings: The performance vocabulary is broadly well-targeted: high-hit keywords like 'low-latency', 'distributed systems', 'profiling', 'multi-threaded', 'CUDA', and 'systems programming' fire on genuine deep-systems job ads, and rare-but-precise terms (eBPF, DPDK, RDMA, JVM internals, MLIR) cleanly identify low-level work. The major false-positive risks are (a) generic depth-claim phrases applied to non-performance topics ('strong knowledge of Page Object Model', 'deep knowledge of math/probability', 'solid grasp of 3NF'), (b) hardware tokens that fire on hardware-validation/firmware roles unrelated to perf (FPGA on test-bench JDs, DRAM on memory-controller test, Verilog on firmware), and (c) abbreviations that collide with other meanings ('P4' as a vendor proficiency level, 'BGP' as a routing protocol that hits networking SREs not internals, 'APM' as generic observability tooling rather than profiling, 'Raft' as a company name, 'codegen' as Swagger/GraphQL Codegen). Several keywords are pure scope-creep (performance improvement/improvements, optimize performance, identifying bottlenecks, scalable systems, massive scale, algorithm design, numerical methods) that fire on ordinary product/engineering work and dilute the topic's claim of 'low-level/internals depth that AI cannot substitute'. Roughly 118/450 keywords are zero-hit, and several strong concepts (networking_internals, distributed_systems_internals, compilers_and_runtimes) carry many never-fired tokens that should be pruned to keep the vocabulary defensible.

process_scaffolding.headline_findings: The list is largely well-formed but contains four catastrophic false-positive keywords driving most of the topic's noise: 'LeSS' (567 hits, almost entirely matching the CSS preprocessor 'LESS' or the word 'less' due to case-insensitive substring matching), 'SAFe' (557 hits, dominated by 'safe operation', 'safe deployment', 'safe and maintainable'), 'ART' (515 hits, almost all 'state of the art' or 'state-of-the-art'), and to a lesser extent 'governance' (1345 hits, heavily contaminated by 'data governance', 'AI/LLM governance', 'cloud governance'). 'Validation' and 'verification' as bare tokens are dominated by data validation, test verification, and identity verification rather than process-level V&V, and 'audit' is contaminated by security/compliance audits unrelated to process governance. 'Specifications' is the dominant Specification-authoring keyword but frequently means API/file format specifications (ELF specs, REST specs) rather than authoring practice. The Requirements concept is dominated by the bare 'requirements' (9514 hits, 49 percent of the sample) which is so generic it acts as a 'job listing has any requirements section' marker rather than requirements engineering. Several genuine gaps exist (refactor 'specifications' to require authorship verbs, add 'ceremonies', 'demos', 'showcases', 'IPM', 'sprint demos', 'agile ceremony').

legacy_stack.headline_findings: The list is broadly accurate but suffers from three structural problems: (1) several keywords are duplicated across concepts within this same topic (VSS, TFVC, BizTalk/BizTalk Server, JAX-WS, JAX-RPC) which double-counts postings and muddles concept attribution; (2) high-volume terms whose modern usage dominates the legacy meaning are silently inflating the topic rate (Active Directory is overwhelmingly Azure AD/Entra in cloud-architect JDs; LDAP/Kerberos/Group Policy commonly co-occur with modern SSO/Okta; SOAP web services and J2EE are routinely listed as one of many integration patterns next to REST/Spring Boot in greenfield JDs; PL/SQL is a current Oracle skill, not a legacy marker; VBA hits are 80%+ Excel macros, not legacy code maintenance; CVS hits include the pharmacy chain); (3) a handful of generic acronyms (IMS, MVS, ESB, OSB, IIB, NTLM, GPO, ESX) carry real polysemy and need keyword guards even though their dominant sense is correct. On the positive side, the core mainframe concept (COBOL/JCL/CICS/VSAM/REXX/IDMS) is well-covered with low FP rate, and several missing legacy markers visible in snippets should be added: Wildfly, MSMQ, Pacbase, RACF, ClearQuest, XenDesktop, Expediter. No keywords have a true zero-hit problem (only Visual SourceSafe at 0, but its abbreviation VSS captures the same postings).

guards.false_positive_pattern: Dominated by 'Azure Active Directory', 'Azure AD', 'Entra ID' mentions in modern cloud-architect JDs; the cloud variant (AAD/Entra) is the opposite of legacy. Several top examples show this: 'Configuring Azure Active Directory Sync', 'azure active directory, ztad, integration with MFA, Okta', 'Azure AD, LDAP, SSO, MFA'.
guards.suggested_guard: Require the 4-token left context to NOT contain 'Azure', 'AAD', 'Entra', or 'cloud'; OR require co-occurrence in the JD with at least one of {on-prem, on premise, domain controller, GPO, Kerberos, LDAP, ADFS} to count as a legacy signal. Drop the bare 'Active Directory' match when 'Azure' appears within 3 tokens.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Frequently appears alongside modern IAM (Okta, Ping Identity, ForgeRock, SSO/MFA, Azure AD) as a still-supported protocol, not as a legacy marker. LDAP is a current standard.
guards.suggested_guard: Treat LDAP as legacy ONLY when co-occurring with on-prem markers (Active Directory non-cloud, OpenLDAP, eDirectory, Tivoli Directory) or when JD uses 'legacy/migrate/maintain'; otherwise downweight or exclude. Consider moving LDAP out of the indicator list entirely since it is a living protocol.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Same issue as LDAP — appears as one item in modern auth-protocol lists ('SAML, OAuth, and Kerberos', 'Hadoop ecosystem (Kerberos)', 'SAML, OAUTH, Kerberos, JWT Token, SSO'). Kerberos is still the standard for AD auth and Hadoop security.
guards.suggested_guard: Require co-occurrence with on-prem AD signals (domain controller, GPO, ADFS, Group Policy) to count as legacy. Drop when listed alongside OAuth/JWT/SAML/OIDC in a generic auth-protocols enumeration.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Some hits are 'group policy' as a generic term ('STIG reviews, group policy and permissions') unrelated to AD GPOs. Other hits are valid (Domain Controller setup, GPO objects).
guards.suggested_guard: Require capitalized 'Group Policy' OR 'GPO' co-occurring with 'Active Directory'/'Domain Controller'/'AD' within the same sentence; exclude standalone 'group policy' lowercase mentions about access policies.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Three-letter acronym; one example shows it grouped with 'AAD / Entra' (modern cloud), and another with 'AWS, and other modern deployment/infrastructure tools' — these are NOT legacy contexts.
guards.suggested_guard: Word-boundary match only ('\bGPO\b'), AND require co-occurrence with 'Active Directory'/'AD'/'Group Policy' in the same JD; exclude when nearest neighbours include 'Entra'/'AAD'/'cloud'.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Both observed examples mention NTLM as one item in a generic Windows-auth list ('Windows Authentication, NTLM, Kerberos, Certificates, PKI'); enumeration-list mentions are weak signals.
guards.suggested_guard: Require NTLM AND at least one of {SharePoint, IIS, Windows Server 200x, on-prem Exchange} in the same JD. With only 2 hits, consider dropping outright.

guards.false_positive_pattern: IMS is also a common company name ('IMS is currently offering an exciting opportunity' in the example) and appears as a US healthcare acronym (Integrated Marketing Solutions, IMS Health). 4 of 5 examples are correct (mainframe IMS DB), but the company-name pattern repeats.
guards.suggested_guard: Require IMS to co-occur in the same JD with at least one of {mainframe, COBOL, DB2, CICS, JCL, z/OS, IMS DB, IMS DC, hierarchical database}; exclude when 'IMS' begins a sentence as a subject ('IMS is...', 'IMS Health').

guards.false_positive_pattern: Three-letter acronym risk; in this sample all hits are legitimate (MVS COBOL, MVS JCL), but downstream the acronym is at risk of matching unrelated tokens.
guards.suggested_guard: Word-boundary match only, AND require co-occurrence with one of {mainframe, COBOL, JCL, z/OS, OS/390, Cobol} within 50 tokens.

guards.false_positive_pattern: ESB is a generic architectural concept; many hits are not products but design patterns ('SOA, ESB concepts', 'ESB, JMS, File/Data transfers'). These can apply to MuleSoft RTF / modern integration platforms in greenfield work.
guards.suggested_guard: Require ESB to co-occur with a specific legacy ESB product name (TIBCO, WebMethods, Oracle Service Bus, BizTalk, IBM Integration Bus, Mule ESB) to count as a legacy marker; otherwise the bare term is too generic.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Three-letter acronym. All current hits are correct (Oracle Service Bus), but at scale the token risks matching other things (Open Source Business, etc.).
guards.suggested_guard: Match only when within 20 tokens of 'Oracle' OR 'Service Bus'; or replace with the unambiguous 'Oracle Service Bus' as the primary surface form.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Three-letter acronym; current hits are correct (IBM Integration Bus) but token alone is too short.
guards.suggested_guard: Word-boundary match AND co-occurrence with 'IBM' OR 'Integration Bus' OR 'Message Broker' within 30 tokens.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Of 51 hits, the dominant pattern is 'Excel VBA' for trading-desk / business-app developers — Excel automation is not 'legacy code maintenance' in the topic-definition sense; it is current-day Microsoft Office scripting.
guards.suggested_guard: Treat VBA as legacy ONLY when co-occurring with 'Visual Basic 6'/'VB6'/'Access 2003-2010'/'legacy'/'classic Visual Basic'; downweight or exclude bare 'Excel VBA' which is current Office work. Alternatively move VBA out of legacy_stack and into a separate Office/Excel-developer signal.

guards.false_positive_pattern: CVS Health (pharmacy chain) is a major US employer — at least 1 of 5 example hits is the company name ('Partner with technical leaders within CVS and the open-source community'). Healthcare and retail JDs will inflate this systematically.
guards.suggested_guard: Require CVS to co-occur with at least one of {SVN, Subversion, Git, ClearCase, version control, source control, repository} in the same JD; exclude when 'CVS Health'/'CVS Pharmacy'/'CVS Caremark' appears anywhere.

adds.concept: Legacy Java EE / app server stack
adds.evidence_snippet: [Lead Software Engineer - Pharmacy Core Platform] '7+ years of experience administering Oracle WebLogic, JBoss or Wildfly application servers'; [Software Engineer III with Security Clearance] 'Experience with Wildfly or JBoss EAP'; [Cloud Site Reliability Engineer] 'like Tomcat or Wildfly'. Wildfly is the modern descendant of JBoss AS and a clear Java-EE app-server signal; appears in at least 8 distinct snippets.

[Associate Engineer, Software Development] 'Experience using WCF, WPF, MSMQ, XML, SOAP, MVC, Unity Framework'. Microsoft Message Queuing is the pre-Service-Bus on-prem Windows messaging stack and fits squarely in the .NET-Framework-era stack.

[Application Developer Supervisor - HYBRID] 'advanced-level computer programming language (for example, COBOL, Delphi, Java, Powerbuilder, Visual Basic, Pl 1, SAS PCS, Pacbase, Full Visual Suite, Designer 2000)'. IBM Pacbase is a discontinued 4GL/code generator with strong legacy signal.

[Senior Mainframe Security Engineer] 'At least 3 years of experience of IBM z/OS including knowledge of RACF, TSO, ISPF. SDSF, and JCL'. RACF is the canonical IBM z/OS security manager and a strong mainframe marker; currently absent.
[Sr. QA Analyst] 'Experience using Rational ClearCase and ClearQuest preferred'. IBM Rational ClearQuest is the change-management companion to ClearCase; legacy ALM tooling.

[Global Virtualization & Cloud Engineer IV] 'Expert-level technical knowledge of VDI technologies, VMware Horizon View, Citrix XenApp and XenDesktop'. List currently has Citrix XenApp and XenServer but not XenDesktop, which is the desktop-VDI counterpart.

[Mainframe Developer] 'IBM or CA tools and utilities (TSO, Expediter, InterTest, Debug, Platinum, Princeton, etc.)'. Compuware Xpediter (also spelled Expediter) is mainframe debug tooling. List has 'Xpediter' (2 hits) but not the alternate spelling 'Expediter'.

concept_redefines.concept: Legacy Microsoft server / collaboration vs Legacy enterprise integration / ESB / messaging vs Legacy version control and build
concept_redefines.issue: Three pairs of keywords (VSS, TFVC, BizTalk/BizTalk Server) are duplicated across concepts inside this single topic, causing the same posting to be counted under two concepts and inflating per-concept hit-rates. The 'Legacy Microsoft server / collaboration' concept currently mixes (a) actual collaboration servers (SharePoint, Lync, InfoPath, Exchange-era), (b) BI/data tools (SSIS, SSRS, SSAS, Crystal Reports), (c) version control (TFS, TFVC, VSS), and (d) integration (BizTalk). These are four distinct semantic clusters.
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Split 'Legacy Microsoft server / collaboration' into: (i) 'Legacy Microsoft collaboration' (SharePoint 2010-2016, Lync, Skype for Business, InfoPath, Exchange on-prem); (ii) 'Legacy Microsoft BI stack' (SSIS, SSRS, SSAS, Crystal Reports — note Crystal Reports is SAP, may belong elsewhere); (iii) move TFS/TFVC/VSS into 'Legacy version control and build' as the canonical home; (iv) move BizTalk/BizTalk Server into 'Legacy enterprise integration / ESB / messaging' as the canonical home. Remove the duplicate entries left behind.

concept_redefines.concept: Legacy enterprise SOAP / web services vs Legacy Java EE / app server stack
concept_redefines.issue: JAX-WS and JAX-RPC are duplicated across both concepts. They are SOAP/WS-* APIs, not app-server features.
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Keep JAX-WS and JAX-RPC only in 'Legacy enterprise SOAP / web services'; remove them from 'Legacy Java EE / app server stack'.

concept_redefines.issue: Three of the four highest-volume keywords in this concept (Active Directory n=202, LDAP n=82, Kerberos n=28, Group Policy n=37) are NOT reliably 'legacy' — they are routinely used in modern hybrid-cloud and on-prem-AD environments, and Active Directory in particular is dominated by 'Azure Active Directory' / 'Entra ID' mentions, which are the OPPOSITE of legacy. The concept currently risks misclassifying ~300 modern cloud-IAM postings as legacy.
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Apply the per-keyword guards above (require on-prem context, exclude Azure/Entra/cloud co-occurrence). Consider redefining the concept as 'On-prem Windows-domain identity stack' and tightening to: ADFS, Tivoli Identity Manager, SiteMinder, OpenLDAP, NTLM (with guard), Kerberos+AD-only context. Move the bare 'LDAP' and 'Kerberos' tokens to a 'protocol' tier with low individual weight.

concept_redefines.issue: VBA's 51 hits are overwhelmingly 'Excel VBA' for trading desks and finance teams — this is current Microsoft Office macro work, not maintenance of pre-2015 enterprise systems. Including it inflates the legacy-language signal with a population of Office-power-user developers who are not legacy maintainers.
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Either (a) split VBA out into a separate 'Office automation' tag and exclude from legacy_stack scoring, or (b) restrict VBA matches to those co-occurring with 'Visual Basic 6'/'VB6'/'Access database'/'legacy'.

concept_redefines.issue: PL/SQL (n=268) is currently the second-highest-hit keyword in the topic, but PL/SQL is the active stored-procedure language for current Oracle Database (19c, 23c). Its presence in a JD does not signal 'legacy database platform' — it signals 'Oracle DB skill', which is mainstream today.
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Remove bare 'PL/SQL' from this concept, OR require co-occurrence with 'Oracle 8i'/'9i'/'10g'/'11g'/'Oracle Forms'/'Oracle Reports' to count as legacy. Without this, PL/SQL alone is a false-positive driver.

concept_redefines.concept: Legacy Java EE / app server stack — J2EE placement
concept_redefines.issue: J2EE (n=413, the topic's largest hit-driver) is often used loosely in JDs as a synonym for 'Java enterprise development' alongside Spring Boot/microservices ('Strong core Java and j2ee skills - backend coding and development', 'Java / J2EE technologies and Spring Framework'). Many of these are greenfield Spring Boot positions where 'J2EE' is a vestigial keyword in the JD template.
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Keep J2EE but downweight; or require J2EE to co-occur with at least one of {WebLogic, WebSphere, JBoss, EJB 2/3, Struts, JSP, Servlets, monolith, mainframe} to count as a strong legacy signal. Same treatment for 'JEE' and 'Java EE'.

context_infrastructure.headline_findings: The vocabulary fires on 371 keywords with zero dead entries, but several high-volume terms drive most of the topic mass through false positives: 'monitoring' (3107 hits), 'metrics' (1476), 'stakeholders' (3994), and 'cross-functional' (3453) routinely match generic language that has nothing to do with observability or stakeholder coordination as defined. The 'Architecture decision records & RFCs' concept is silently absorbed by 'technical specifications' (419 hits) and 'design artifacts' (83), which in snippets overwhelmingly mean requirements-to-spec translation and generic design deliverables, not deliberative ADR/RFC artifacts. The 'Data-pipeline & data-integration hygiene' concept conflates hygiene/governance language (lineage, contracts, dictionaries) with raw tool names (snowflake, redshift, databricks, ETL) that signal data-engineering roles rather than hygiene practice. Several keywords are unambiguous false positives ('Notion' matching 'notion of', 'Sentry' matching Tesla 'sentry mode', 'instrumentation' matching industrial PLC instrumentation, 'playbooks' matching Ansible playbooks, 'protobuf' as a wire format rather than API doc, 'ability to write' matching 'ability to write SQL/code').

drops.reason: Of 4 inspected snippets only 2 referenced the Notion product; the rest matched 'notion of security best practices' / 'notion of having... participants' (philosophical sense). Recall is too low to justify the FP rate.

Matches Tesla 'sentry mode' and 'sentry' in unrelated contexts; among 47 hits at least one is a Tesla-internship FP and recall is duplicative with 'datadog'/'newrelic' for the same observability signal.
Snippets show generic 'architecture and design artifacts for complex applications' or 'design artifacts, test plans/cases' boilerplate, not deliberative ADR/RFC output. Concept-misaligned.
26 hits dominated by sales/SOW 'technical proposal decks', 'technical proposal preparation, compliance matrix' (govt contracting). Not engineering-internal RFC artifacts.
Same FP pattern as 'technical proposals' — sales/contracting bid proposals dominate.
Inspected snippets show 'translating business specs to technical specs' (requirements engineering) and mainframe 'technical specs, UTP/UTR' (deliverable docs), not RFC-style proposals. Misaligned.
Two snippets: one is requirements 'UAT, requirements and tech specs' (BA-style), one fits. Hit count of 2 not worth the noise.
Snippets all show 'translate requirements into technical specification' — requirements-engineering/SDLC artifact, not ADR/RFC proposal. Same FP pattern as plural form.
Both snippets match QA/test 'design review process providing expertise in testing requirements', not architectural deliberation.

Snippets show industrial 'PLCs, motors, pumps, valves, sensors' and 'Field instrumentation' (controls/SCADA) and Android 'Instrumentation and debugging' (test framework). True observability sense is minority.

362 hits, but inspected snippets are 'ability to write efficient queries', 'ability to write robust code in Python', 'ability to write and execute advanced SQL'. Almost never refers to writing prose/docs.
Protobuf is a serialization/wire format technology, not an API documentation artifact. Snippets confirm: 'JSON, XML, Protobuf', 'Protobuf / gRPC'. Belongs to a different topic.
Snippets show 'JSON, JSON Schema, REST, OAuth2' (data validation) and 'Pydantic, Zod, or JSON Schema' (output validation), not interface documentation.
Snippets show 'GraphQL schema design and implementation' — schema design is a coding/architecture activity, not interface documentation.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Bare 'monitoring' co-occurs with admin/maintenance senses: 'Maintenance and Support activities monitoring, reporting', 'Monitoring and improving front-end performance', 'monitoring & alerting' as IT-ops boilerplate. Drives 3107 hits with high noise.
guards.suggested_guard: Require co-occurrence within a small window (~6 tokens) with one of: logging|alerting|observability|metrics|telemetry|dashboards|traces|logs|prometheus|datadog|grafana|splunk|application performance — i.e., only count 'monitoring' when adjacent to a clear observability collocate.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Catches 'business metrics', 'quality metrics', 'reliability and performance metrics', 'KPIs/metrics' that belong to product-literacy or generic-quality senses, not observability stack.
guards.suggested_guard: Exclude when preceded by business|product|quality|engineering|customer|user|adoption|hiring|HR|recruiting|growth|financial; or require co-occurrence with logs|traces|dashboards|prometheus|grafana|datadog|telemetry|instrumentation within ~10 tokens.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Most snippets are clean ('metrics, logs and traces'), but bare 'logs' can match 'audit logs', 'application logs' debugging, or 'CICS dumps and traces'.
guards.suggested_guard: Require co-occurrence with metrics|traces|observability|aggregation|centralized|structured|telemetry|elastic|kibana|splunk|fluentd within ~8 tokens; otherwise downweight.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Matches 'sniffer traces' (network packets), 'CICS dumps and traces' (mainframe debugging), unrelated to distributed tracing.
guards.suggested_guard: Require co-occurrence with metrics|logs|distributed|opentelemetry|jaeger|honeycomb|datadog|tempo within ~8 tokens.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Matches Workday 'Alerts and Notifications framework', Salesforce 'alerts', security 'custom alerts' for SOC playbooks — not necessarily observability alerting.
guards.suggested_guard: Require co-occurrence with monitoring|alerting|metrics|threshold|pagerduty|opsgenie|on-call|incident within ~8 tokens; exclude when adjacent to 'notifications' as part of Workday/Salesforce framework names.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Bare 'ELK' is OK in observed snippets but the token can theoretically match unrelated abbreviations; mostly fine. Lower priority guard.
guards.suggested_guard: Require uppercase ELK as whole token AND nearby Elasticsearch|Logstash|Kibana|stack|observability|logging within ~12 tokens.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Acronym; could match Asset Performance Management, Application Portfolio Management. Inspected snippets cleanly mean Application Performance Monitoring.
guards.suggested_guard: Require co-occurrence with monitoring|tools|Dynatrace|Datadog|AppDynamics|NewRelic|tracing within ~10 tokens to disambiguate from Asset/Portfolio Management.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Top hit is 'Ansible playbooks, roles, and modules' — configuration-management code, not operational docs. Roughly half the inspected snippets are Ansible.
guards.suggested_guard: Exclude when preceded within 4 tokens by Ansible|ansible; otherwise count. Add separate match for 'support playbooks'|'incident playbooks'|'runbook' co-occurrence to recover the operational sense.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Often denotes mere on-call expectation ('willing to provide on-call support evenings and weekends') rather than operational doc/runbook authoring.
guards.suggested_guard: This concept includes on-call participation as a context-infra signal, but if the goal is documentation specifically, require co-occurrence with rotation|runbook|incident|postmortem|playbook|escalation within ~10 tokens to keep it on-concept; otherwise consider routing it to a different concept (operational ownership) or accepting it with a note.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Matches generic 'SLA Compliance, productivity', 'cases as assigned in an acceptable SLA' (helpdesk ticket SLA) — not SRE reliability targets.
guards.suggested_guard: Require co-occurrence with SLO|SLI|error budget|reliability|uptime|availability|MTTR|service level objective within ~12 tokens; otherwise treat as generic ticketing-SLA noise.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Same as 'SLA' — frequently 'service level agreements' for support cases, not reliability targets.
guards.suggested_guard: Same guard as 'SLA' (require SLO|SLI|error budget|reliability|MTTR|uptime collocate).

guards.false_positive_pattern: Matches company name 'KPI' ('Azure Cloud is being used by KPI'), and generic 'KPI objectives' boilerplate; also routinely refers to engineering-system KPIs (data-system reliability) rather than product/business literacy.
guards.suggested_guard: Require co-occurrence with business|product|customer|growth|revenue|adoption|user|company|strategic within ~6 tokens; exclude when used as proper noun (capitalized, not followed by lowercase context word).

guards.false_positive_pattern: Same FP pattern: 'KPIs related to security incidents, response times' or 'support KPIs' that are operational not business-literacy.
guards.suggested_guard: Same as 'KPI'; require business|product|customer|strategic|growth collocate.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Most snippets describe 'user-centric design' / 'user-centric interfaces' — UI/UX craft language rather than product-literacy/business-context awareness.
guards.suggested_guard: Exclude when followed within 3 tokens by design|interface|interfaces|UI|UX; or require co-occurrence with needs|outcomes|empathy|research|insights to keep the literacy sense.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Frequently appears in 'directly impact key business metrics' boilerplate without any actual literacy content.
guards.suggested_guard: Acceptable as-is for breadth, but consider requiring co-occurrence with understand|drive|track|measure|analyze|connect within ~6 tokens to filter pure aspiration boilerplate.

guards.false_positive_pattern: 3994 hits with extreme breadth; matches generic 'update stakeholders', 'business stakeholders' as filler. Drives most of topic mass.
guards.suggested_guard: Keep but downweight; or require co-occurrence with manage|align|partner|engage|communicate|translate|non-technical|cross-functional within ~6 tokens to retain the coordination-skill sense vs. mere stakeholder-presence boilerplate.

adds.evidence_snippet: Senior Cloud Engineer: 'Document SOPs and help...' — 'standard operating procedures' is a direct synonym for runbooks/playbooks and appears unprompted.
Implied by SOP usage; full form not currently in vocabulary. Standard SRE term.
Software Engineer II - CTJ-POLY: 'improves troubleshooting guides (TSGs), wikis, tests, and telemetry to make on-call better' — explicit term for troubleshooting guides as operational doc.
Principal Application Engineer (SRE): 'Prognosis, Web ViewPoint, Grafana, Kibana, Datadog, AppDynamics' — major APM vendor missing from list.
QA Automation Engineer: 'application monitoring and logging tools like Datadog and CloudWatch' — AWS-native observability tool, frequent in postings, not in list.
Sr. Information Security Engineer: 'using a SIEM (Splunk, Sentinel, etc) to perform analysis, threat hunting, and incident response' — observability adjacent log-analytics platform.

Data Engineer II: 'production-quality pipeline documentation skills: data lineage, transformation logic, and design decision logs' — explicit hygiene practice; current list has 'data lineage' but not the active practice phrase.

Principal Backend Engineer: 'Collaborate with cross-functional teams to translate business requirements into robust technical solutions' — recurring phrase capturing translation skill.
Already in list (305 hits) — confirming high signal; no add needed (validation only).
Solutions Architect: 'Being a conduit between the GTM (Go to Market) Team and Product/Engineering team' — current list has 'GTM' but not full form.

Technical Program Manager abbreviation appears in cross-functional descriptions; alongside 'work with PM/PMs' patterns, TPM is a common counterpart not in the list. Validate before adding — recommend search before commit.

Senior Software Engineer: 'Leading and contributing to architectural decisions, technical design reviews and cross-team collaboration' — singular 'design review' captures the architectural-deliberation sense better than the requirements-only 'technical specification' patterns.

concept_redefines.issue: The concept is currently dominated by 'technical specifications' (419), 'design artifacts' (83), and 'architecture reviews' (71) — terms whose snippets show requirements-to-spec translation, generic SDLC deliverables, and code-quality reviews. The deliberative-artifact sense (ADR/RFC) is genuinely rare in postings (ADRs: 10, RFCs: 10, architecture decision records: 5). The hit_rate of 3.5% is therefore inflated.
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Either (a) tighten the concept to true ADR/RFC artifacts and accept a real concept_hit_rate near 1%, dropping 'technical specification(s)/proposal(s)/specs' as drops above; or (b) rename the concept to 'Design proposals & deliberative artifacts' and explicitly include design docs, design reviews, and engineering proposals as in-scope while still removing the requirements-translation snippets via guards.

concept_redefines.issue: The concept mixes hygiene/governance language ('data quality', 'data lineage', 'data contracts', 'data dictionaries', 'data stewardship', 'data observability') with raw data-engineering tool nouns ('snowflake' 794, 'databricks' 657, 'redshift' 399, 'bigquery' 267, 'airflow' 525, 'dbt' 224). The latter primarily mark data-engineering job postings rather than hygiene-as-a-skill, and they collectively dominate the concept's hit_rate.
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Split the concept into (a) 'Data hygiene & governance practices' (lineage, contracts, validation, dictionaries, stewardship, quality checks, observability, catalog, mesh) and (b) 'Data platform tools' (snowflake, databricks, redshift, bigquery, airflow, dbt, dagster, prefect, lakehouse). The hygiene concept then yields a clean rate-of-practice signal; the tools concept can be kept as proxy if needed but should not be conflated with hygiene.

concept_redefines.issue: Two of the largest keywords ('stakeholders' 3994, 'cross-functional' 3453) are filler boilerplate. Many postings include them without any specific coordination practice, inflating concept_hit_rate to 36% — likely the highest-noise concept in the topic.
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Either (a) add proximity guards (require stakeholders/cross-functional to be within ~6 tokens of manage|align|partner|translate|non-technical|communicate to count); or (b) downgrade 'stakeholders' and 'cross-functional' to count-with-half-weight relative to more specific phrases ('non-technical stakeholders', 'translate technical concepts', 'partner with product/design', 'cross-functional partners') so the concept is anchored on coordination acts rather than mere mention.

concept_redefines.issue: 'ability to write' (362 hits) and 'written communication' (1123 hits) drive most of this concept; 'ability to write' almost always refers to writing code/SQL, not prose. After dropping 'ability to write', 'written communication' alone may be too generic to distinguish 'writing craft' from generic communication.
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Drop 'ability to write' (already in drops). Either rename concept to 'Written communication' and accept the broader scope, or guard 'written communication' to require collocates (clear|excellent|strong|technical|design|documents) to keep the craft framing. Without this, the concept and 'Cross-functional communication' overlap heavily on 'written communication'.

concept_redefines.issue: Concept is partially hijacked by general API/serialization tech rather than documentation practice. 'protobuf', 'GraphQL schema', 'JSON schema' are about interface implementation, not docs. After drops, the concept hit_rate (1.8%) will fall further.
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Tighten concept to documentation artifacts only (Swagger, OpenAPI, API docs/specs/specifications/references/contracts, developer portals/Backstage, Postman collections, interface documentation). Accept lower hit_rate as a true reflection of how rarely postings call out API-doc authoring as a skill.

concept_redefines.issue: Bare-token keywords ('monitoring', 'metrics', 'logs', 'logging', 'alerts', 'traces', 'instrumentation') account for the majority of the concept's hit volume but each carries notable FP patterns (industrial controls, Workday alerts, mainframe traces, business metrics).
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Adopt the per-keyword proximity guards listed above. Additionally, consider distinguishing between 'observability platform usage' (vendor names — high precision) and 'observability practice language' (bare verbs/nouns — needs context window). Reporting both subscores would make the concept more interpretable.

design_artifacts_cluster.description: ADR / PRD / design doc / spec doc as written artifacts
design_artifacts_cluster.rationale: Artifacts are the substrate; orchestration is about authoring the work, process_scaffolding is about governance. The artifact noun lives with the substrate.

spec_authoring_activity.description: Writing / decomposing / authoring specs as an activity
spec_authoring_activity.rationale: Authoring activity for AI/agent consumption is the new senior archetype the paper tracks; classical SDLC verbs ('requirements gathering', 'change request') stay in process_scaffolding.

code_review.rationale: Code review's primary JD framing is correctness gating. 'Mentor through code review' / 'use reviews to teach' stays in mentorship as a more specific phrase, but bare 'code review' belongs in verification.

design_review.rationale: Design/architecture review is most often framed as a governance/quality artifact-process. Mentorship version captures 'teach through architecture review' — keep there as specific phrase only.

observability.rationale: Observability as a substrate (dashboards, telemetry hygiene) lives in context_infrastructure. Verification claims 'post-deployment observability for catching regressions' — a specific framing, keep narrowly. Performance claims 'profiling/perf telemetry' — a specific framing, keep narrowly.

leadership_vs_management.rationale: These are role/seniority titles, not mentorship verbs. Mentorship should keep the verbs ('mentor junior engineers', 'grow the team') and yield the title nouns to people_management — but note that 'lead' is highly polysemous and needs a separate guard.
